div.head: The Secretary of State to the Minister in Liberia (Walton)
div.p: 50 [bis]. Your 80, September 2, 10 a.m., first paragraph. The Department desires that you ascertain by thorough investigation whether the practices referred to in your despatch no. 646Dated July 10, p. 527. have actually been discontinued. If any such practices still exist, you should submit by mail full particulars, including names of parties involved.In despatch No. 665, September 17, the Minister in Liberia reported that a thorough investigation on this subject was not productive of tangible results (740.00112 European War 1939/3667). The Department also desires specific information in support of your statement that the British Legation is refusing to grant export permits for shipments of piassava from Liberia to the United States while granting permits for shipments to England.
